paa_questions: How much does a boiler repair cost in London?

pages.example: When a boiler breaks down in Hammersmith in January, it's not an inconvenience — it's an emergency. We get calls from Fulham and Chiswick too, usually within hours of the temperature dropping. Nine times out of ten, we can fix it the same day.

paa_questions: How often should a boiler be serviced?

Does servicing a boiler extend its life?
Do I need a boiler service every year by law?
pages.example: We service a lot of boilers in Camden Town and Kentish Town every autumn — homeowners who want to get ahead of winter before the first cold week hits. A boiler that's been serviced runs more efficiently, breaks down less, and lasts longer.

paa_questions: What certificates do I need after a new boiler is installed?

How long does a new boiler warranty last?
pages.example: A lot of the new boiler jobs we do in Kensington and Chelsea are replacements that were overdue by three or four years. The old boiler was still running but costing a fortune. A modern condensing boiler uses significantly less gas for the same heat output.

paa_questions: What is a power flush and do I need one?

How often should a central heating system be power flushed?
pages.example: We did a power flush on a Victorian terrace in Maida Vale last spring — seven radiators, half of them stone cold at the bottom. After the flush, the whole system heated up evenly within 20 minutes.

pages.example: We install a lot of Nest and Hive thermostats across West Hampstead and Hampstead — mostly for homeowners who want to stop heating empty rooms. The setup takes about an hour and most people are controlling their heating from their phone before we leave.

paa_questions: How do I bleed a radiator?

pages.example: A lot of the radiator calls we get from Kilburn and Brondesbury are for TRVs that have seized up over summer. The radiator looks fine, the boiler is working, but one room stays cold. Nine times out of ten it's a stuck thermostatic valve.

paa_questions: Why is my central heating making a banging noise?

pages.example: A noisy heating system in a Marylebone flat turned out to be a failing pump — the kind of problem that gets worse slowly until it stops working entirely on the coldest night of the year. Catching it early saved the homeowner a more expensive emergency callout.

pages.example: We fit a lot of showers across Paddington and Maida Vale — conversions from bath-only bathrooms and replacements for units that have given up. Most electric shower installations take half a day.

pages.example: A dripping tap in Willesden Green was wasting close to 5,000 litres of water a month. The homeowner had been ignoring it for a year. A new cartridge and 45 minutes of our time fixed it.

paa_questions: What should I do if I smell gas at home?

pages.example: If you can smell gas in your Westminster or Kensington home, don't wait. Open windows, don't use switches, and call the National Gas Emergency line on 0800 111 999 first, then us. We're Gas Safe registered and can attend the same day.

pages.example: We cover a lot of rental properties across the London Borough of Brent and Willesden Green — mostly for letting agents who need CP12 certificates before a new tenancy starts. We can usually fit a same-week appointment and issue the certificate on the day.

pages.example: A new gas hob in a Notting Hill kitchen renovation sounds straightforward until you discover the existing gas point is in the wrong place. We handle gas meter relocations, new gas point installations, and full appliance fitting — all Gas Safe registered.
